chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The making up for the Month of Ramazan

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/312)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/312)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّنْ يَقْضِي شَهْرَ رَمَضَانَ مُنْقَطِعاً قَالَ إِذَا حَفِظَ أَيَّامَهُ فَلَا بَأْسَ.

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the one who makes up for a Month of Ramazan with breaks (discontinues manner). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he has memorised his (number of missed) days, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah, who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who fasts Qada’ (compensatory fast) for the fast of the month of Ramadan in separate times. He (the Imam) said, Tt is not harmful if he keeps the number of days without missing.’”
